description_long: This bungalow features a single-story rectangular structure with a stone-textured exterior and a prominent gable roof covered in gray shingles. The front facade includes a small porch with steps leading up to a main entrance, flanked by several evenly spaced windows. The roof extends over the porch, providing shelter and architectural interest. The side and rear elevations show fewer windows, with the back having a more utilitarian appearance and a small window near the corner. The building sits on a flat, grassy plot with a few vertical panels or fences on one side, suggesting a boundary or property line. The overall design is simple and functional, typical of suburban or residential neighborhoods.
description: A single-story stone bungalow with a gray gable roof, front porch, and evenly spaced windows, set on a grassy plot.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance, porch, and a row of windows along the facade.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the roof, porch extension, and partial visibility of the front and side windows.
description_view_2: Rear view with minimal windows and a more utilitarian appearance, showing the back door and a small window.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with the porch partially visible and a clear look at the side wall and window arrangement.
